# Plex Generate Previews

> GPU-accelerated video preview thumbnail (BIF) generation for Plex Media Server. Uses FFmpeg hardware decoding (CUDA, VAAPI, D3D11VA, VideoToolbox) and parallel workers to generate previews 5-10x faster than Plex's built-in method. Distributed as a Docker image with a web UI as the only interface (no CLI).

## Quick Start

Run with Docker (Intel/AMD GPU via VAAPI):

```
docker run -d \
  --name plex-generate-previews \
  --restart unless-stopped \
  -p 8080:8080 \
  --device /dev/dri:/dev/dri \
  -e PUID=1000 -e PGID=1000 \
  -v /path/to/media:/media:ro \
  -v /path/to/plex/config:/plex:rw \
  -v /path/to/app/config:/config:rw \
  stevezzau/plex_generate_vid_previews:latest
```

For NVIDIA, replace `--device` with `--gpus all` and add `-e NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all`. Use `all` rather than `compute,video,utility`: the `graphics` capability (included in `all`) is what makes the NVIDIA Container Toolkit inject the NVIDIA Vulkan driver, which is required for Dolby Vision Profile 5 thumbnails (libplacebo Vulkan tone-mapping).

Open `http://YOUR_IP:8080`, get the auth token from `docker logs plex-generate-previews | grep "Token:"`, and complete the setup wizard.

## Features

- Multi-GPU support: NVIDIA (CUDA), AMD (VAAPI), Intel (QuickSync/VAAPI), Windows (D3D11VA), macOS (VideoToolbox)
- Per-GPU configuration in Settings: enable/disable individual GPUs, workers per GPU, FFmpeg threads per GPU
- GPU-to-CPU fallback workers for unsupported codecs
- Web UI (only interface): dashboard for job management, scheduling, and settings
- Cron and interval-based scheduling
- Radarr/Sonarr/Tdarr webhook integration for automatic processing on media import
- Custom webhook endpoint for any external tool
- Library filtering to process specific Plex libraries
- Adjustable thumbnail quality (1-10) and frame interval (1-60s)
- Plex OAuth sign-in via setup wizard
- Path mapping support for Docker volume differences
- REST API with token authentication
- WebSocket real-time progress updates
- Rate limiting and CORS configuration

## Volume Mounts

- `/media` (ro) ‚Äî media files
- `/plex` (rw) ‚Äî Plex application data where BIF files are stored
- `/config` (rw) ‚Äî app settings, schedules, job history

## GPU Support

| GPU      | Platform | Acceleration     | Docker Flag              |
|----------|----------|------------------|--------------------------|
| NVIDIA   | Linux    | CUDA/NVENC       | `--gpus all`             |
| AMD      | Linux    | VAAPI            | `--device /dev/dri`      |
| Intel    | Linux    | QuickSync/VAAPI  | `--device /dev/dri`      |
| NVIDIA   | Windows  | CUDA             | Native only              |
| AMD/Intel| Windows  | D3D11VA          | Native only              |
| Apple    | macOS    | VideoToolbox     | Native only              |

## Configuration

**settings.json** at `/config/settings.json` is the sole source of truth. On first start, env vars are migrated as seed values. After that, configure via the Web UI Settings page.

**Infrastructure env vars** (always active): `CONFIG_DIR`, `WEB_PORT`, `PUID`, `PGID`, `TZ`, `CORS_ORIGINS`, `HTTPS`, `DEV_RELOAD`, `WEB_AUTH_TOKEN`

**Deprecated** (use Settings instead): `GPU_SELECTION`, `GPU_THREADS`, `FFMPEG_THREADS`

**Per-GPU config**: In Settings ‚Üí Processing Options, configure each GPU: enabled, workers, ffmpeg_threads.

## REST API

All endpoints require `X-Auth-Token` or `Authorization: Bearer` header except `/api/health` and `/api/setup/status`.

Key endpoints:
- `GET /api/health` ‚Äî health check (no auth)
- `GET /api/setup/status` ‚Äî setup completion status (no auth)
- `GET /api/settings` ‚Äî current settings
- `POST /api/settings` ‚Äî update settings (partial)
- `GET /api/jobs` ‚Äî list jobs
- `POST /api/jobs` ‚Äî create job (`{"library_id": "1", "library_name": "Movies"}`)
- `GET /api/jobs/{id}` ‚Äî job details with worker status
- `POST /api/jobs/{id}/cancel` ‚Äî cancel job
- `GET /api/schedules` ‚Äî list schedules
- `POST /api/schedules` ‚Äî create schedule (cron or interval)
- `POST /api/webhooks/radarr` ‚Äî Radarr webhook receiver
- `POST /api/webhooks/sonarr` ‚Äî Sonarr webhook receiver
- `POST /api/webhooks/custom` ‚Äî custom webhook (`{"file_path": "..."}` or `{"file_paths": [...]}`)
- `GET /api/webhooks/history` ‚Äî recent webhook events
- `GET /api/system/status` ‚Äî system status (GPUs, workers, job counts)
- `GET /api/libraries` ‚Äî Plex libraries
- `POST /api/processing/pause` ‚Äî pause all processing
- `POST /api/processing/resume` ‚Äî resume processing

## WebSocket

Real-time updates via Socket.IO on the `/jobs` namespace. Events: `job_progress`, `job_complete`, `job_error`, `worker_update`.
